message:
fix: validate line structure before skipping in mdn-data patch

The `i += 2` line skip after finding a `createRequire` line assumed an
exact upstream file structure without validation. If css-tree changes
its data.js layout, the script would silently skip unrelated lines and
corrupt the file.

Now each of the two subsequent lines is validated against the expected
`require('mdn-data/...')` pattern before being skipped. Mismatches
produce a clear warning with expected vs actual content, and only
confirmed matches are skipped.

Also adds post-patch verification: the `didPatch` flag previously only
confirmed that `createRequire` was found, not that JSON data was
actually inlined. The script now checks that all three inlined variable
declarations (mdnAtrules, mdnProperties, mdnSyntaxes) exist in the
output and that no residual `require()` calls remain, aborting the
write with a warning if verification fails.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
